name: python-igraph (Fast Graph Analysis Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-2.0); Gábor Csárdi and igraph team

auth.notes: Local library; C extension (igraph C library)

performance.notes: C-backed; orders of magnitude faster than NetworkX for large graphs

agent_readiness.notes: C-backed graph analysis. from igraph import Graph. g = Graph(directed=True). g.add_vertices(5). g.add_edges([(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)]). g.degree(), g.betweenness(), g.closeness(). g.shortest_paths(0, 4). g.pagerank(). g.community_louvain(). g.community_edge_betweenness(). g.are_connected(0, 3). g.subgraph([0,1,2]). g.get_adjacency(). Graph.Famous('Petersen'). Graph.Erdos_Renyi(100, 0.05). Layout: g.layout_fruchterman_reingold(). Plot: from igraph import plot; plot(g, 'graph.png'). Read/write: g.write_graphml('graph.graphml'). Graph.Read_GraphML(). VS(vertex_seq), ES(edge_seq) for attribute assignment.

gotchas: GPL-2.0 — copyleft; commercial use requires compliance
C extension — compilation required; prebuilt wheels available on PyPI
API is different from NetworkX — not a drop-in replacement
Vertex/edge naming is by integer index — string IDs need mapping
best_when: Large-scale graph analysis (millions of nodes) where NetworkX is too slow
Graph algorithms: PageRank, betweenness centrality, shortest paths at scale
R-igraph users migrating to Python — same underlying C library
avoid_when: Graph databases (use Neo4j driver) — igraph is in-memory only
Simple graph tasks where NetworkX's API is preferred
summary: python-igraph provides fast C-backed graph analysis. Graph(directed=True).add_vertices().add_edges(). betweenness(), pagerank(), shortest_paths(), community_louvain(). Reads/writes GraphML, GML, etc. GPL-2.0 by igraph team. Type-annotated. Best for large-scale graph analysis where NetworkX is too slow. Not a NetworkX drop-in. Integer vertex IDs. Community detection algorithms included.
